metaDescription: Outsourced controller services for US and Canadian companies. A senior controller embedded full-time to own your month-end close, reporting, and controls.

hero.lede: An outsourced controller, sometimes called a fractional controller, is a senior finance professional who owns your month-end close, reporting, and controls. Satinwood Talent embeds one full-time in your business, with the seniority of a local hire, at a fraction of the cost.
hero.credLine: Serving the US and Canada · CA Sri Lanka, ACCA, CIMA, CFA qualified

atAGlance.v: A fraction of a local hire

work.title: What an outsourced controller does.
work.lede: A controller owns the integrity of your numbers, not just the data entry. A Satinwood Talent controller runs the following end to end, inside your own tools.

items.title: Month-end close
items.body: Owns the full close on a fixed cadence, with reconciliations done and the books locked on time, every month.

items.title: Financial reporting
items.body: Builds the monthly reporting pack leadership and investors can trust, with the judgment behind the numbers.

items.title: Controls and compliance
items.body: Sets and runs the internal controls that keep the numbers clean and the business audit-ready.

items.title: Cash and working capital
items.body: Tracks cash, AR, AP, and working capital so you always know your position and runway.

items.title: Budgeting and forecasting
items.body: Supports the budget and forecast, and the variance analysis that explains what actually happened.

items.title: Audit and raise prep
items.body: Gets the books and the file ready for an audit, a raise, or diligence, without the last-minute scramble.

why.title: A controller who owns your numbers.

items.title: Always senior
items.body: Controller level and above, qualified and experienced, with real ownership inside global firms.

items.title: Dedicated and embedded
items.body: Full-time and exclusive to you, inside your tools and team. Not a shared pool and not a pooled task queue.

items.title: Finance only
items.body: We place finance and nothing else. Most offshore providers are generalist marketplaces. We are a finance firm.

items.title: A fraction of the cost
items.body: The same seniority as a local controller, for far less, because the talent sits in a lower-cost market, not because the work is junior.

compare.titleLead: The seniority of a local hire,
compare.titleEm: at a fraction of the cost.

rows.label: Relative cost
rows.sat: A fraction of a local hire

whoFor.title: Built for growing US and Canadian companies.

items.title: Your first finance leader
items.body: You have outgrown a bookkeeper and need someone to own the numbers, but it is not time for a full-time controller's salary.

items.title: Replacing a departing controller
items.body: Your controller left and the close cannot wait two or three months for a local hire to start.

items.title: Scaling past spreadsheets
items.body: The business has gotten complex and the reporting and controls need a senior hand to hold the standard.

items.title: Getting ready to raise or sell
items.body: You need clean books and an audit-ready file before diligence, and you need it handled now.

items.a: An outsourced controller is a senior finance professional who owns your month-end close, reconciliations, financial controls, and reporting from outside your local payroll. With Satinwood Talent the controller is embedded full-time in your business and your tools, so you get ownership of the numbers, not a task queue. It is the same seniority as a local controller, at a fraction of the cost.

items.q: What does an outsourced controller do?
items.a: A controller owns the integrity of your numbers. That means running the month-end close, reconciliations, the financial reporting pack, cash and working-capital reporting, budgeting and forecasting support, internal controls, and audit preparation. Satinwood Talent controllers run this end to end inside your own tools.

items.q: What is the difference between an outsourced controller and a bookkeeper?
items.a: A bookkeeper records transactions; a controller owns the numbers. A bookkeeper handles data entry, AP, and AR. A controller owns the close, the controls, the reporting package, and the judgment behind the figures. Satinwood Talent places controller-level talent, not junior bookkeeping.

items.q: Outsourced controller or fractional CFO, which do I need?
items.a: A controller owns the accuracy and the close; a CFO owns strategy, capital, and the board. Most growing companies need the close owned long before they need a full-time CFO. A Satinwood Talent controller gives you reliable numbers and clean reporting, and you add CFO-level help when the business calls for it.

items.q: Is an outsourced controller the same as a fractional controller?
items.a: Close. A fractional controller usually means part-time hours; an outsourced controller can be part-time or full-time, often from a lower-cost market. Satinwood Talent places a full-time, dedicated controller, so you get full ownership of the close and controls, not a few hours a week.

items.q: Can an offshore controller run our month-end close?
items.a: Yes. A senior offshore controller can own your full month-end close, reconciliations, settlements, and the monthly reporting pack, working inside your own tools, with hours that overlap your day so the close never waits.

items.q: How senior are Satinwood Talent's controllers?
items.a: Controller level and above. Operators are CA Sri Lanka, ACCA, CIMA, or CFA qualified, with real ownership experience inside global firms, Big Four, and listed groups. The average senior operator on our bench has 15 or more years of experience.

items.q: Is the controller dedicated and full-time, or shared?
items.a: Dedicated, full-time, and exclusive to your business, embedded in your tools and your team. Not a shared pool, not a pooled task queue, not a marketplace match.

items.q: How much does an outsourced controller cost compared with a local hire?
items.a: A fraction of a local controller, who in the US or Canada typically runs well into six figures with benefits. You get the same seniority and ownership for far less, because the talent sits in a lower-cost market, not because the work is junior.

items.q: Do you work with US and Canadian companies?
items.a: Yes. Satinwood Talent serves growing companies across the United States and Canada, as well as the United Kingdom, the United Arab Emirates, and Australia. Operators work hours that overlap your day, agreed up front.

items.q: How fast can you place a controller, and is there a trial?
items.a: About two weeks from the first call to a hand-selected senior match. Every match starts on a trial to confirm fit, and if it is not right we replace it. Terms are flexible and rolling, with no long lock-in.

items.q: How is our financial data handled?
items.a: Secure, company-issued devices, signed NDAs, role-based access, and documented handling standards. Your financial data is treated as if our own books depended on it.
